texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 1: FIFA WORLD CUP ™
texts.text: Article 1: FIFA WORLD CUP ™

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.1 The FIFA World Cup™ is a FIFA competition embodied in the FIFA Statutes.
texts.text: 1.1 The FIFA World Cup™ is a FIFA competition embodied in the FIFA Statutes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.2 The FIFA World Cup takes place every four years. As a general rule, every association affiliated to FIFA may participate in the FIFA World Cup.
texts.text: 1.2 The FIFA World Cup takes place every four years. As a general rule, every association affiliated to FIFA may participate in the FIFA World Cup.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.3 Participation in the FIFA World Cup is free of charge.
texts.text: 1.3 Participation in the FIFA World Cup is free of charge.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.4 The FIFA World Cup 26™ consists of a preliminary competition and a final competition.
texts.text: 1.4 The FIFA World Cup 26™ consists of a preliminary competition and a final competition.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.5 The final competition ("FIFA World Cup 26") is scheduled to be played from 11 June to 19 July 2026.
texts.text: 1.5 The final competition ("FIFA World Cup 26") is scheduled to be played from 11 June to 19 July 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.6 Any rights associated with the FIFA World Cup 26 that are not granted by these Regulations for the FIFA World Cup 26™ ("Regulations") and/or further regulations, guidelines and decisions as set forth in these Regulations and/or specific agreements to a Participating Member Association in the preliminary or final competition, or to a confederation, shall remain with and/or belong to FIFA.
texts.text: 1.6 Any rights associated with the FIFA World Cup 26 that are not granted by these Regulations for the FIFA World Cup 26™ ("Regulations") and/or further regulations, guidelines and decisions as set forth in these Regulations and/or specific agreements to a Participating Member Association in the preliminary or final competition, or to a confederation, shall remain with and/or belong to FIFA.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.7 These Regulations govern the rights, duties and responsibilities of all member associations taking part in the FIFA World Cup 26 ("Regulations"). The Regulations and all directives, decisions, guidelines and circulars issued by FIFA in connection with the FIFA World Cup 26 are binding on all parties participating and involved in the preparation, organisation and hosting of the FIFA World Cup 26.
texts.text: 1.7 These Regulations govern the rights, duties and responsibilities of all member associations taking part in the FIFA World Cup 26 ("Regulations"). The Regulations and all directives, decisions, guidelines and circulars issued by FIFA in connection with the FIFA World Cup 26 are binding on all parties participating and involved in the preparation, organisation and hosting of the FIFA World Cup 26.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.8 The FIFA Statutes and all FIFA regulations in force shall apply to and be binding on all parties participating and involved in the preparation, organisation and hosting of the FIFA World Cup 26. Any reference in these Regulations to the FIFA Statutes and regulations refers to the FIFA Statutes and regulations valid at the time of application.
texts.text: 1.8 The FIFA Statutes and all FIFA regulations in force shall apply to and be binding on all parties participating and involved in the preparation, organisation and hosting of the FIFA World Cup 26. Any reference in these Regulations to the FIFA Statutes and regulations refers to the FIFA Statutes and regulations valid at the time of application.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1.9 Other rules and/or decisions that are directly binding on FIFA (e.g. due to FIFA being a signatory to the World Anti‑Doping Code) may apply to the subject matter of these Regulations. Any such rules and/or decisions implemented by FIFA shall take precedence over these Regulations in the event of any conflict and shall be complied with by all parties participating and involved in the preparation, organisation and hosting of the FIFA World Cup 26™.
texts.text: 1.9 Other rules and/or decisions that are directly binding on FIFA (e.g. due to FIFA being a signatory to the World Anti‑Doping Code) may apply to the subject matter of these Regulations. Any such rules and/or decisions implemented by FIFA shall take precedence over these Regulations in the event of any conflict and shall be complied with by all parties participating and involved in the preparation, organisation and hosting of the FIFA World Cup 26™.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 2: PRELIMINARY COMPETITION
texts.text: Article 2: PRELIMINARY COMPETITION

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.1 The preliminary competition shall be organised by FIFA in collaboration with the confederations. The confederations have established a competition format, which received the required approval from FIFA.
texts.text: 2.1 The preliminary competition shall be organised by FIFA in collaboration with the confederations. The confederations have established a competition format, which received the required approval from FIFA.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.2 On entering the preliminary competition, each member association:
texts.text: 2.2 On entering the preliminary competition, each member association:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) acknowledges and agrees that all administrative matters related to the preliminary competition shall be dealt with by the respective confederation in compliance with the regulations submitted, and FIFA will only intervene if a confederation requests FIFA's assistance in those matters; and
texts.text: acknowledges and agrees that all administrative matters related to the preliminary competition shall be dealt with by the respective confederation in compliance with the regulations submitted, and FIFA will only intervene if a confederation requests FIFA's assistance in those matters; and

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) undertakes to observe the principles of fair play.
texts.text: undertakes to observe the principles of fair play.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.3 The FIFA World Cup 26™ Play‑Off Tournament ("Play‑Off Tournament") is considered to be part of the preliminary competition. It is scheduled to be played within an existing window of the Men's International Match Calendar (23 to 31 March 2026). The Regulations for the FIFA World Cup 26™ Play‑Off Tournament are published by FIFA.
texts.text: 2.3 The FIFA World Cup 26™ Play‑Off Tournament ("Play‑Off Tournament") is considered to be part of the preliminary competition. It is scheduled to be played within an existing window of the Men's International Match Calendar (23 to 31 March 2026). The Regulations for the FIFA World Cup 26™ Play‑Off Tournament are published by FIFA.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.4 In the event that any of the confederations' preliminary competitions are not held or completed in a manner that determines which member association(s) qualify for the final competition (due to cancellation, postponement, abandonment or otherwise), FIFA shall decide, after consultation with the applicable confederation, the member association(s) based within the territorial region of the confederation as the participating member association(s).
texts.text: 2.4 In the event that any of the confederations' preliminary competitions are not held or completed in a manner that determines which member association(s) qualify for the final competition (due to cancellation, postponement, abandonment or otherwise), FIFA shall decide, after consultation with the applicable confederation, the member association(s) based within the territorial region of the confederation as the participating member association(s).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The confederation shall base its proposal strictly on the sporting merits of the member association(s) and shall provide a written report to FIFA explaining the reasons for proposing the member association(s) when seeking FIFA's approval. In the event that FIFA does not approve the confederation's proposed member association(s), the confederation shall propose an alternative member association(s) for FIFA's approval.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 3: FIFA ORGANISING BODY
texts.text: Article 3: FIFA ORGANISING BODY

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3.1 The FIFA Council or the relevant committee appointed by the FIFA Council is responsible for organising the FIFA World Cup 26 in accordance with these Regulations, the FIFA Statutes, the FIFA Governance Regulations as well as the FIFA Media and Marketing Regulations.
texts.text: 3.1 The FIFA Council or the relevant committee appointed by the FIFA Council is responsible for organising the FIFA World Cup 26 in accordance with these Regulations, the FIFA Statutes, the FIFA Governance Regulations as well as the FIFA Media and Marketing Regulations.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3.2 The FIFA Council or the Bureau of the FIFA Council, may, if necessary, appoint a sub‑committee to deal with unforeseen matters that require an immediate decision. Any decision taken by the Bureau of the FIFA Council, or by the sub‑committee, shall come into effect immediately but shall be subject to confirmation by the FIFA Council.
texts.text: 3.2 The FIFA Council or the Bureau of the FIFA Council, may, if necessary, appoint a sub‑committee to deal with unforeseen matters that require an immediate decision. texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3.3 The FIFA Council or the relevant committee shall deal with any aspect of the FIFA World Cup 26 that is not the responsibility of any other body under the terms of these Regulations, the FIFA Statutes, the FIFA Governance Regulations or the FIFA Media and Marketing Regulations.
texts.text: 3.3 The FIFA Council or the relevant committee shall deal with any aspect of the FIFA World Cup 26 that is not the responsibility of any other body under the terms of these Regulations, the FIFA Statutes, the FIFA Governance Regulations or the FIFA Media and Marketing Regulations.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3.4 The decisions taken by the FIFA Council or the relevant committee and/or its sub‑committee in relation to the FIFA World Cup 26 may be appealed, pursuant to the FIFA Statutes.
texts.text: 3.4 The decisions taken by the FIFA Council or the relevant committee and/or its sub‑committee in relation to the FIFA World Cup 26 may be appealed, pursuant to the FIFA Statutes.

texts.label: list_item

texts.text: 3.5 FIFA bears overall responsibility for organising, hosting and staging the FIFA World Cup 26.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4.1 FIFA has established local FIFA subsidiaries, namely FWC26 Canada Football Ltd, FWC2026 Mexico S. de R.I de C.V and FWC2026 US, Inc. with offices based in Toronto, Canada, Mexico City, Mexico, and Miami, United States (each a "Local FIFA Subsidiary"), in order to deliver various operational projects relating to the FIFA World Cup 26.
texts.text: 4.1 FIFA has established local FIFA subsidiaries, namely FWC26 Canada Football Ltd, FWC2026 Mexico S. de R.I de C.V and FWC2026 US, Inc. with offices based in Toronto, Canada, Mexico City, Mexico, and Miami, United States (each a "Local FIFA Subsidiary"), in order to deliver various operational projects relating to the FIFA World Cup 26.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4.2 Each Local FIFA Subsidiary may communicate with the Participating Member Associations from time to time on matters relating to the FIFA World Cup 26. Notwithstanding the foregoing, FIFA remains the ultimate authority and responsible party on all operational and competition matters relating to the organisation, hosting and staging of the FIFA World Cup 26.
texts.text: 4.2 Each Local FIFA Subsidiary may communicate with the Participating Member Associations from time to time on matters relating to the FIFA World Cup 26. Notwithstanding the foregoing, FIFA remains the ultimate authority and responsible party on all operational and competition matters relating to the organisation, hosting and staging of the FIFA World Cup 26.

texts.orig: Article 6: WITHDRAWAL, UNPLAYED MATCHES, ABANDONED MATCHES AND REPLACEMENT
texts.text: Article 6: WITHDRAWAL, UNPLAYED MATCHES, ABANDONED MATCHES AND REPLACEMENT

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6.1 All Participating Member Associations undertake to play all of their matches until eliminated from the FIFA World Cup 26.
texts.text: 6.1 All Participating Member Associations undertake to play all of their matches until eliminated from the FIFA World Cup 26.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6.2 Any Participating Member Association that withdraws from the FIFA World Cup 26 no later than 30 days before the first match of the final competition shall be fined at least CHF 250,000 by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee. Any Participating Member Association that withdraws from the FIFA World Cup 26 fewer than 30 days before the first match of the final competition shall be fined at least CHF 500,000 by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee.
texts.text: 6.2 Any Participating Member Association that withdraws from the FIFA World Cup 26 no later than 30 days before the first match of the final competition shall be fined at least CHF 250,000 by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee. texts.label: text

texts.text: Participating Member Associations that withdraw from the FIFA World Cup 26 at any time will be required to reimburse the team preparation money as well as any other tournament‑related contribution payments that they received from FIFA.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If any Participating Member Association is not admitted to, is excluded from or withdraws from the FIFA World Cup 26 once the tournament has started, the FIFA Disciplinary Committee may impose additional disciplinary measures. These may take into account, in particular, the timing of the withdrawal or exclusion, the severity of the rule breach leading to non‑admission or exclusion, possible mitigating factors as well as any other relevant circumstances.

texts.text: 6.5 If a Participating Member Association withdraws or a match cannot be played or is abandoned as a result of force majeure, the authorised FIFA organising body (including the Tournament Operation Centre) shall decide on the matter at its sole discretion and take whatever action is deemed necessary.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6.6 Further to the above provision, in the case of a match being abandoned as a result of force majeure after it has already kicked off, the following principles shall apply:
texts.text: 6.6 Further to the above provision, in the case of a match being abandoned as a result of force majeure after it has already kicked off, the following principles shall apply:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) The match shall recommence at the minute at which play was interrupted rather than being replayed in full, and with the same scoreline. The match shall recommence where play was stopped when the match was interrupted (e.g. with a free kick, throw‑in, goal kick, corner kick, penalty kick, etc.). If the match was abandoned while the ball was still in play, it shall restart with a dropped ball from the position of the ball when play was stopped.
texts.text: The match shall recommence at the minute at which play was interrupted rather than being replayed in full, and with the same scoreline. The match shall recommence where play was stopped when the match was interrupted (e.g. with a free kick, throw‑in, goal kick, corner kick, penalty kick, etc.). If the match was abandoned while the ball was still in play, it shall restart with a dropped ball from the position of the ball when play was stopped.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) The match shall recommence with the same players on the pitch and substitutes available as when the match was abandoned.
texts.text: The match shall recommence with the same players on the pitch and substitutes available as when the match was abandoned.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) No additional substitutes may be added to the list of players on the start list.
texts.text: No additional substitutes may be added to the list of players on the start list.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: d) The teams can make only the number of substitutions to which they were still entitled when the match was abandoned.
texts.text: The teams can make only the number of substitutions to which they were still entitled when the match was abandoned.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: e) Players sent off during the abandoned match cannot be replaced.
texts.text: Players sent off during the abandoned match cannot be replaced.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: f) Any disciplinary measures imposed before the match was abandoned will be dealt with in accordance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code.
texts.text: Any disciplinary measures imposed before the match was abandoned will be dealt with in accordance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: g) The kick‑off time, date, location and any other matter shall be decided by FIFA.
texts.text: The kick‑off time, date, location and any other matter shall be decided by FIFA.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 7.1 Disciplinary infringements are dealt with in compliance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code in force as well as with all relevant circulars and directives, with which the Participating Member Associations and Delegation Members undertake to comply.
texts.text: 7.1 Disciplinary infringements are dealt with in compliance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code in force as well as with all relevant circulars and directives, with which the Participating Member Associations and Delegation Members undertake to comply.

texts.label: list_item

texts.text: 7.3 Any violations of these Regulations or any other FIFA regulations, circulars, guidelines, directives and/or decisions that do not come under the jurisdiction of another FIFA body shall be dealt with by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee in accordance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code.

texts.orig: Article 8: DISPUTE RESOLUTION
texts.text: Article 8: DISPUTE RESOLUTION

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 8.1 All disputes in connection with the FIFA World Cup 26 shall be promptly settled by negotiation (with the exception of those falling under art. 7 above).
texts.text: 8.1 All disputes in connection with the FIFA World Cup 26 shall be promptly settled by negotiation (with the exception of those falling under art. 7 above).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 8.2 As provided for in the FIFA Statutes, Participating Member Associations and their Delegation Members may not take disputes to an ordinary court of law but to the exclusive jurisdiction of FIFA.
texts.text: 8.2 As provided for in the FIFA Statutes, Participating Member Associations and their Delegation Members may not take disputes to an ordinary court of law but to the exclusive jurisdiction of FIFA.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 8.3 The Participating Member Associations and all Delegation Members acknowledge and accept that, once all internal channels have been exhausted at FIFA, their sole legal recourse shall be to the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) in Lausanne, Switzerland, as provided for in the FIFA Statutes, and unless excluded by the FIFA Statutes or applicable FIFA regulations. Any such arbitration proceedings shall be governed by the CAS Code of Sports‑related Arbitration.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 9: PROTESTS
texts.text: Article 9: PROTESTS

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.1 For the purpose of these Regulations, protests are objections of any kind related to events or matters that have a direct effect on matches, including but not limited to the state of and markings on the pitch, accessory match equipment, eligibility of players, stadium installations and footballs.
texts.text: 9.1 For the purpose of these Regulations, protests are objections of any kind related to events or matters that have a direct effect on matches, including but not limited to the state of and markings on the pitch, accessory match equipment, eligibility of players, stadium installations and footballs.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.2 Unless otherwise stipulated in this article, protests shall be submitted in writing to the FIFA Match Director within two hours of the match in question, as well as via the FIFA Legal Portal at legalportal.fifa.com (cf. art. 18 par. 1 of the FIFA Disciplinary Code) within 24 hours of the end of the match, otherwise they shall be disregarded.
texts.text: 9.2 Unless otherwise stipulated in this article, protests shall be submitted in writing to the FIFA Match Director within two hours of the match in question, as well as via the FIFA Legal Portal at legalportal.fifa.com (cf. art. 18 par. 1 of the FIFA Disciplinary Code) within 24 hours of the end of the match, otherwise they shall be disregarded.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.3 Protests regarding the eligibility of players nominated for matches shall be submitted via the FIFA Legal Portal at legalportal.fifa.com no later than five days before the first match of the final competition and shall be dealt with by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee.
texts.text: 9.3 Protests regarding the eligibility of players nominated for matches shall be submitted via the FIFA Legal Portal at legalportal.fifa.com no later than five days before the first match of the final competition and shall be dealt with by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.4 Protests regarding the state of the pitch, its surroundings, markings or accessory items (e.g. goals, flag posts or footballs) shall be made in writing to the referee before the start of the match by the head of delegation of the team lodging the protest. If the pitch's playing surface becomes unplayable during a match, the captain of the protesting team shall immediately lodge a protest with the referee in the presence of the captain of the opposing team. The protests shall be confirmed in writing to the FIFA Match Director by the head of the team delegation no later than two hours after the match.
texts.text: 9.4 Protests regarding the state of the pitch, its surroundings, markings or accessory items (e.g. goals, flag posts or footballs) shall be made in writing to the referee before the start of the match by the head of delegation of the team lodging the protest. If the pitch's playing surface becomes unplayable during a match, the captain of the protesting team shall immediately lodge a protest with the referee in the presence of the captain of the opposing team. The protests shall be confirmed in writing to the FIFA Match Director by the head of the team delegation no later than two hours after the match.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.5 Protests against any incidents that occur during the course of a match shall be made to the referee by the team captain immediately after the disputed incident and before play has resumed, in the presence of the captain of the opposing team. The protest shall be confirmed in writing to the FIFA Match Director by the head of delegation no later than two hours after the match.
texts.text: 9.5 Protests against any incidents that occur during the course of a match shall be made to the referee by the team captain immediately after the disputed incident and before play has resumed, in the presence of the captain of the opposing team. The protest shall be confirmed in writing to the FIFA Match Director by the head of delegation no later than two hours after the match.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.6 No protests may be made about the referee's decisions regarding facts connected with play. Such decisions are final and not subject to appeal, unless otherwise stipulated in the FIFA Disciplinary Code.
texts.text: 9.6 No protests may be made about the referee's decisions regarding facts connected with play. Such decisions are final and not subject to appeal, unless otherwise stipulated in the FIFA Disciplinary Code.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.7 If an unfounded or irresponsible protest is lodged, the FIFA Disciplinary Committee may impose disciplinary measures against the Participating Member Association concerned.
texts.text: 9.7 If an unfounded or irresponsible protest is lodged, the FIFA Disciplinary Committee may impose disciplinary measures against the Participating Member Association concerned.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.8 If any of the formal conditions of a protest as set out in these Regulations (and in the FIFA Disciplinary Code, where applicable) are not met, such protest shall be disregarded by the competent body. Once the final match of the FIFA World Cup 26 has ended, any protests described in this article shall be disregarded. Notwithstanding the above, the FIFA Disciplinary Committee remains competent to prosecute any disciplinary infringement ex officio, as established in the FIFA Disciplinary Code.
texts.text: 9.8 If any of the formal conditions of a protest as set out in these Regulations (and in the FIFA Disciplinary Code, where applicable) are not met, such protest shall be disregarded by the competent body. Once the final match of the FIFA World Cup 26 has ended, any protests described in this article shall be disregarded. Notwithstanding the above, the FIFA Disciplinary Committee remains competent to prosecute any disciplinary infringement ex officio, as established in the FIFA Disciplinary Code.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9.9 FIFA shall pass decisions on any protests lodged, subject to the exceptions stipulated in these Regulations, the FIFA Statutes or any other FIFA regulations.
texts.text: 9.9 FIFA shall pass decisions on any protests lodged, subject to the exceptions stipulated in these Regulations, the FIFA Statutes or any other FIFA regulations.

texts.orig: Article 10: YELLOW AND RED CARDS
texts.text: Article 10: YELLOW AND RED CARDS

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10.1 The referee has the authority to take disciplinary action from entering the field of play for the pre‑match inspection until leaving the field of play after the match ends (including penalties (penalty shoot‑out)). If, before entering the field of play at the start of the match, a player or team official commits a sending‑off offence, the referee has the authority to prevent the player or team official from taking part in the match (see Laws of the Game, Law 12.3, or any relevant amendment); the referee will report any other misconduct.
texts.text: 10.1 The referee has the authority to take disciplinary action from entering the field of play for the pre‑match inspection until leaving the field of play after the match ends (including penalties (penalty shoot‑out)). If, before entering the field of play at the start of the match, a player or team official commits a sending‑off offence, the referee has the authority to prevent the player or team official from taking part in the match (see Laws of the Game, Law 12.3, or any relevant amendment); the referee will report any other misconduct.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10.2 Single yellow cards and pending suspensions as a consequence of cautions in different matches in the preliminary competition are not carried over to the final competition. Pending match suspensions imposed as a result of a red card in matches in the preliminary competition are carried over to the final competition.
texts.text: 10.2 Single yellow cards and pending suspensions as a consequence of cautions in different matches in the preliminary competition are not carried over to the final competition. Pending match suspensions imposed as a result of a red card in matches in the preliminary competition are carried over to the final competition.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10.3 Single yellow cards in the final competition will be cancelled after the quarter‑finals.
texts.text: 10.3 Single yellow cards in the final competition will be cancelled after the quarter‑finals.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10.4 If a player or team official receives two cautions during two different matches, they will automatically be suspended from their team's subsequent match.
texts.text: 10.4 If a player or team official receives two cautions during two different matches, they will automatically be suspended from their team's subsequent match.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10.5 If a player or team official is sent off as a result of a direct or indirect red card (second caution), they will automatically be suspended from their team's subsequent match. In addition, further sanctions may be imposed.
texts.text: 10.5 If a player or team official is sent off as a result of a direct or indirect red card (second caution), they will automatically be suspended from their team's subsequent match. In addition, further sanctions may be imposed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10.6 Any suspension that cannot be served during the FIFA World Cup 26 will be carried over to the representative team's next official match in accordance with the relevant provisions of the FIFA Disciplinary Code.
texts.text: 10.6 Any suspension that cannot be served during the FIFA World Cup 26 will be carried over to the representative team's next official match in accordance with the relevant provisions of the FIFA Disciplinary Code.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 14.1 In the knockout stages, if a match is level at the end of normal playing time, extra time shall be played. The players shall remain on the pitch during both of these intervals.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 18.1 Each team participating in the FIFA World Cup 26 shall arrive in the host country no later than five days before its first group match in the respective host country.
texts.text: 18.1 Each team participating in the FIFA World Cup 26 shall arrive in the host country no later than five days before its first group match in the respective host country.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 18.2 Only official team accommodation in Canada, Mexico and the USA (Team Base Camp Hotels and Venue‑Specific Team Hotels) designated as such by FIFA shall be used for the teams' accommodation from five days before the team's first match and up to one day after its elimination or last match in the FIFA World Cup 26.
texts.text: 18.2 Only official team accommodation in Canada, Mexico and the USA (Team Base Camp Hotels and Venue‑Specific Team Hotels) designated as such by FIFA shall be used for the teams' accommodation from five days before the team's first match and up to one day after its elimination or last match in the FIFA World Cup 26.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 18.3 Each team participating in the FIFA World Cup 26 shall choose a Team Base Camp from the Team Base Camp brochure, as per the official Team Base Camp selection policy, which shall be its main accommodation and training base until the knockout stages begin (i.e. round of 32). Each team shall travel from its Team Base Camp to the match venue one day before matchday (MD‑1) and in exceptional cases on MD‑2, and shall return to their Team Base Camp after the match (on MD/MD+1).

texts.label: list_item

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 19.1 Matches shall be played on natural grass. For natural grass pitches, FIFA's guidelines and requirements shall be followed. In order to ensure that the field of play is of the highest quality, it shall not be used for a non‑football event as of two months before the first match held in the stadium in connection with the FIFA World Cup 26. It shall also not be used for any kind of event as of one month before the first match held in the stadium in connection with the FIFA World Cup 26. Any exemption to these deadlines is subject to FIFA's explicit prior approval in writing.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 20.1 If a stadium has a retractable roof, the FIFA Match Director shall decide before the match whether the roof shall be open or closed during the match. This decision must be announced by the FIFA Match Director at the Match Coordination Meeting.
texts.text: 20.1 If a stadium has a retractable roof, the FIFA Match Director shall decide before the match whether the roof shall be open or closed during the match. This decision must be announced by the FIFA Match Director at the Match Coordination Meeting.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 20.2 If the match starts with the roof open and there is a serious deterioration in the weather conditions impacting the match, the referee, in consultation with the FIFA Match Director, has the authority to order its closure during the match. In such an event, the roof is to remain closed until the end of the match. The roof may only be opened or closed if there is no one on the pitch.

Further details on the submission of the provisional release list, and the deadline by which the list shall be submitted to FIFA, will be stipulated in the relevant circular letter.

Each Participating Member Association shall adhere to the visa requirements and process.

texts.label: list_item

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article 24: FINAL RELEASE LIST
texts.text: Article 24: FINAL RELEASE LIST

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 24.1 Each Participating Member Association shall provide FIFA with a final release list of at least 23 players up to a maximum of 26 players (at least three of whom shall be goalkeepers) and up to 27 team officials (hereinafter "final release list") by the deadline stipulated in the relevant circular letter. This final release list shall contain only players who were named on the provisional release list. Any exception must be approved by FIFA. A signed copy of the final release list shall also be sent to FIFA by the same deadline. All information requested online about the players should be duly completed.
texts.text: 24.1 Each Participating Member Association shall provide FIFA with a final release list of at least 23 players up to a maximum of 26 players (at least three of whom shall be goalkeepers) and up to 27 team officials (hereinafter "final release list") by the deadline stipulated in the relevant circular letter. This final release list shall contain only players who were named on the provisional release list. Any exception must be approved by FIFA. A signed copy of the final release list shall also be sent to FIFA by the same deadline. All information requested online about the players should be duly completed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 24.2 A player listed on the final release list may be replaced with a player from the provisional release list only in the event of serious injury or illness up until 24 hours before the kick‑off of their team's first match in the FIFA World Cup 26. Any exceptions must be approved by FIFA. The replacement shall be nominated by the Participating Member Association, who shall inform FIFA accordingly. Upon receipt and acceptance of a written detailed medical assessment in one of the official FIFA languages, the FIFA Medical Committee, represented by the FIFA Tournament Medical Lead, will ascertain whether the injury or illness is sufficiently serious to prevent the player from taking part in the FIFA World Cup 26. Injured players who are replaced shall return their accreditation to FIFA. Accordingly, players who have returned their accreditation shall no longer be considered members of the Participating Member Association's final release list. The replacement player must be assigned the shirt number of the injured player being replaced.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 26.1 FIFA shall issue each player and team official included on the final release list with an official accreditation bearing a photograph. Each Participating Member Association agrees to fully comply, and to ensure that all members of its delegation, including players, coaches, managers, officials and representatives also comply, with the FIFA Accreditation Terms and Conditions. Each Participating Member Association will receive accreditations in accordance with the final release list. Any additional accreditations may be requested from FIFA Team Services in accordance with the official procedure prior to the final competition. For the sake of clarity, FIFA will only cover the costs of up to a maximum of 50 Delegation Members, as stipulated in the financial provisions of these Regulations.

texts.text: Article 27: TEAM SAFEGUARDING/WELFARE OFFICER

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Each Participating Member Association shall appoint a designated Team Safeguarding/ Welfare Officer to act as a focal point for all matters related to the safeguarding and well‑being of the players and team delegation. This role does not have to be exclusive and can be assigned to a team medical doctor or other trained member of the accredited team delegation. It is a mandatory requirement that the designated Team Safeguarding/Welfare Officer complete the FIFA Guardians Safeguarding Essentials: Open Learners online course (https://safeguardinginfootball.fifa.com/ global‑football‑community‑learners/) and submit the certificate of completion to FIFA. In addition, FIFA recommends that all members of the team delegation accompanying the players complete the course. The role of the Team Safeguarding/ Welfare Officer is to:
texts.text: Each Participating Member Association shall appoint a designated Team Safeguarding/ Welfare Officer to act as a focal point for all matters related to the safeguarding and well‑being of the players and team delegation. This role does not have to be exclusive and can be assigned to a team medical doctor or other trained member of the accredited team delegation. In addition, FIFA recommends that all members of the team delegation accompanying the players complete the course. The role of the Team Safeguarding/ Welfare Officer is to:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: d) liaise with the tournament Safeguarding Manager and the FIFA Event Safeguarding Manager should a concern or an allegation of harassment or abuse (be it psychological, physical or sexual, or neglect) arise;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 28.1 The FIFA Equipment Regulations and the FIFA Media and Marketing Regulations in force apply to all matches of the final competition, unless specified otherwise in these Regulations. To the extent that these Regulations are inconsistent with any aspect of the FIFA Equipment Regulations, the FIFA Equipment Regulations shall take precedence.

To the extent that these Regulations are inconsistent with any aspect of the FIFA Equipment Regulations, the FIFA Equipment Regulations shall take precedence.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The display of political, religious, or personal messages or slogans of any nature in any language or form by players and team officials on their playing, team kits or other clothing considered as outerwear and formal attire, before and after playing, and equipment (including kit bags, beverage containers, medical bags, etc.) or body is prohibited. The similar display of commercial messages and slogans of any nature in any language or form by players and officials is not allowed for the duration of their time at any official activity organised by FIFA (including official matches and training sessions, as well as during official press conferences and mixed‑zone activities).
texts.text: The display of political, religious, or personal messages or slogans of any nature in any language or form by players and team officials on their playing, team kits or other clothing considered as outerwear and formal attire, before and after playing, and equipment (including kit bags, beverage containers, medical bags, etc.) or body is prohibited. texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 28.2 Each team shall inform FIFA of a minimum of two different and contrasting (one predominantly dark and one predominantly light) colours for each of its first‑choice and alternative team kits (shirt, shorts and socks). In addition, each team shall select a minimum of three contrasting colours for the goalkeepers. These three goalkeeper kits must be different and contrasting from each other as well as from the first‑choice and alternative team playing kits. This information shall be submitted online via the team colour form by the stipulated deadline. Only these colours, once reviewed and approved by FIFA as stated in article 28 paragraph 3 below, may be worn during matches.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 28.3 The Participating Member Associations shall send to FIFA for approval a sample of all items of the full first‑choice and alternative team playing kits (shirt, shorts, socks, all three goalkeeper's kits, caps, wristbands and headbands, etc.) intended for use during the final competition. In addition, FIFA shall organise a kit‑day session prior to the final competition; should it do so, the date shall be confirmed by circular letter or other official communication. Representatives of all Participating Member Associations shall attend. All equipment (kits, bags, medical equipment, etc.) that could be on display in the stadium, the training sites and the hotels, or during transfers to, from or within the three host countries, shall be approved by FIFA.

texts.orig: FIFA shall issue a written team equipment inspection report approving or rejecting the items. Should any article of the team kit or any part thereof not be in accordance with the FIFA Equipment Regulations and/or the FIFA Media and Marketing Regulations, the Participating Member Association will be obliged to change it accordingly and resubmit the corrected items within the timeline set in the written team equipment inspection report. Any such decisions are not subject to appeal.

These numbers shall be displayed at chest height on the front of the shirt, on the front of the shorts and on the back of the shirts according to the FIFA Equipment Regulations. The numbers shall correspond to the numbers indicated on the final release list.

texts.text: 29.2 During the final competition, for any goalkeeper replacement, the incoming goalkeeper shall be assigned the next possible shirt number not being used yet, e.g. 27.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 29.3 The player's last name or popular (or abbreviation) name shall be affixed above the number on the back of the shirt and shall be clearly legible in accordance with the FIFA Equipment Regulations. The name on the shirt shall bear a strong resemblance to the player's popular name as indicated on the final release list and in any other official FIFA documentation. In the event of two or more players having the same name on the back of the shirt, further letter(s) or initial(s) shall be added until a clear distinction can be made between the players concerned. In case of doubt, FIFA shall have the final decision regarding the name that appears on the shirt.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 30.1 No later than one month before the start of the final competition, FIFA will inform the teams of the colours that they shall wear for each match of the group stage. As far as is possible, FIFA's preference is for each team to wear its first‑choice colours as declared on the team colour form. If the two teams' colours and the match officials' colour might cause confusion, the following principles shall be applied by FIFA to allocate the match colours:

As far as is possible, FIFA's preference is for each team to wear its first‑choice colours as declared on the team colour form. If the two teams' colours and the match officials' colour might cause confusion, the following principles shall be applied by FIFA to allocate the match colours:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Order of priority from 1 to 5:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If the above order of priority does not produce a clear colour contrast of the kits (having considered the respective first‑choice playing kit and alternative playing kit colour options), the allocation shall continue step by step in reverse order (team B colours first, followed by team A colours and/or reversal of team A and team B assignment, if necessary) until an acceptable colour contrast is confirmed.
texts.text: If the above order of priority does not produce a clear colour contrast of the kits (having considered the respective first‑choice playing kit and alternative playing kit colour options), the allocation shall continue step by step in reverse order (team B colours first, followed by team A colours and/or reversal of team A and team B assignment, if necessary) until an acceptable colour contrast is confirmed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) If necessary, one or both teams may have to wear a combination of their first‑choice and alternative playing or goalkeeper kits, or each team may have to play in its alternative colours, irrespective of its team A or team B status. FIFA will endeavour to ensure that each team wears its first‑choice team playing kit at least once during the group stage. texts.orig: Article 32: START LIST
texts.text: Article 32: START LIST

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 32.1 The electronic start list system will be available and accessible eight hours prior to the kick‑off of the respective match with the full names and shirt numbers of the maximum of 26 players listed on the final release list, together with the full names of the officials eligible to be seated on the team bench. A maximum of 26 players (including a minimum of two goalkeepers) may be chosen for the start list. Changes can be made at the team's discretion up to 90 minutes before kick‑off. The traditional paper start list will also be available prior to each match.
texts.text: 32.1 The electronic start list system will be available and accessible eight hours prior to the kick‑off of the respective match with the full names and shirt numbers of the maximum of 26 players listed on the final release list, together with the full names of the officials eligible to be seated on the team bench. A maximum of 26 players (including a minimum of two goalkeepers) may be chosen for the start list. Changes can be made at the team's discretion up to 90 minutes before kick‑off. The traditional paper start list will also be available prior to each match.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 32.2 The start list should be duly completed by selecting the 11 players who will start the match and the maximum of 15 substitutes, indicating the captain, selecting the officials to be seated on the team bench (maximum of 11) and submitted by the authorised team official and/or signed if applicable. Both teams shall submit their completed start list at least 90 minutes before kick‑off. If the start list is not submitted on time for any reason the matter will be reported to the FIFA Disciplinary Committee. Both teams' start lists will only be published once both teams have submitted them, fully completed.
texts.text: 32.2 The start list should be duly completed by selecting the 11 players who will start the match and the maximum of 15 substitutes, indicating the captain, selecting the officials to be seated on the team bench (maximum of 11) and submitted by the authorised team official and/or signed if applicable. Both teams shall submit their completed start list at least 90 minutes before kick‑off. If the start list is not submitted on time for any reason the matter will be reported to the FIFA Disciplinary Committee. Both teams' start lists will only be published once both teams have submitted them, fully completed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 32.3 At the Match Coordination Meeting (before each match), the teams will receive a start list with the full names and shirt numbers of up to 26 players together with the full names of the team officials eligible to be seated on the bench.
texts.text: 32.3 At the Match Coordination Meeting (before each match), the teams will receive a start list with the full names and shirt numbers of up to 26 players together with the full names of the team officials eligible to be seated on the bench.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 32.4 The numbers on the players' shirts must correspond to the numbers indicated on the start list. Each team is responsible for ensuring that the start list is completed properly and that only the 11 selected players start the match.
texts.text: 32.4 The numbers on the players' shirts must correspond to the numbers indicated on the start list. Each team is responsible for ensuring that the start list is completed properly and that only the 11 selected players start the match.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 32.5 If, after the submission of the start list, any of the 11 players are not able to begin the match due to injury or illness, they may be replaced by any of the eligible substitutes as long as the FIFA Match Director and the referee are officially informed thereof prior to kick‑off. Within 24 hours, the team concerned shall also provide FIFA with a medical report issued by a team medical doctor responsible (in one of the three official FIFA languages).
texts.text: 32.5 If, after the submission of the start list, any of the 11 players are not able to begin the match due to injury or illness, they may be replaced by any of the eligible substitutes as long as the FIFA Match Director and the referee are officially informed thereof prior to kick‑off. Within 24 hours, the team concerned shall also provide FIFA with a medical report issued by a team medical doctor responsible (in one of the three official FIFA languages).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 32.6 Further to the above, any injured or ill player who is removed from the start list will no longer be eligible to take part in the match and thus cannot be selected as a substitute player at any time during the match. Such a change to the start list will not reduce the number of official substitutions that may be made by a team during the match. Although no longer eligible to play as a substitute, the injured or ill player who was removed from the start list before kick‑off may be seated on the team bench and would in that case also be eligible for doping control selection.
texts.text: 32.6 Further to the above, any injured or ill player who is removed from the start list will no longer be eligible to take part in the match and thus cannot be selected as a substitute player at any time during the match. Such a change to the start list will not reduce the number of official substitutions that may be made by a team during the match. Although no longer eligible to play as a substitute, the injured or ill player who was removed from the start list before kick‑off may be seated on the team bench and would in that case also be eligible for doping control selection.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 32.7 Only the players who were identified on the official start list submitted to the FIFA Match Director, or were confirmed as a warm‑up injury/illness replacement player, may start the match. In the case of any discrepancies of players on the pitch at the beginning of the match, the matter will be submitted to the FIFA Disciplinary Committee for a decision.
texts.text: 32.7 Only the players who were identified on the official start list submitted to the FIFA Match Director, or were confirmed as a warm‑up injury/illness replacement player, may start the match. In the case of any discrepancies of players on the pitch at the beginning of the match, the matter will be submitted to the FIFA Disciplinary Committee for a decision.

texts.text: Article 33: TECHNICAL AND WARM‑UP AREAS

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 33.1 The technical area is the area which the coach, other team officials and substitutes are allowed to occupy during the match. It includes the dugout (if applicable), the team bench and a marked zone adjacent to the pitch.
texts.text: 33.1 The technical area is the area which the coach, other team officials and substitutes are allowed to occupy during the match. It includes the dugout (if applicable), the team bench and a marked zone adjacent to the pitch.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 33.2 No more than 26 people (up to 15 substitute players and up to 11 team officials – one of these officials shall be a team medical doctor) shall be allowed to sit on the team bench. A suspended player or team official will not be allowed to sit on the team bench or be in the technical area during the match.
texts.text: 33.2 No more than 26 people (up to 15 substitute players and up to 11 team officials – one of these officials shall be a team medical doctor) shall be allowed to sit on the team bench. A suspended player or team official will not be allowed to sit on the team bench or be in the technical area during the match.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 33.3 Up to 20 additional technical overflow seats (including one seat for the team liaison officer and one seat for the team security liaison officer) in the stands shall be allocated for accredited officials providing technical support to the team during the match (kit manager, assistant physiotherapist, etc.). Staff occupying such seats shall have access to the dressing rooms with a valid accreditation.
texts.text: 33.3 Up to 20 additional technical overflow seats (including one seat for the team liaison officer and one seat for the team security liaison officer) in the stands shall be allocated for accredited officials providing technical support to the team during the match (kit manager, assistant physiotherapist, etc.). Staff occupying such seats shall have access to the dressing rooms with a valid accreditation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 33.4 During the match, substitutes are allowed to leave the technical area to warm up. At the Match Coordination Meeting, the FIFA Match Director shall explain exactly where they may warm up.
texts.text: 33.4 During the match, substitutes are allowed to leave the technical area to warm up. At the Match Coordination Meeting, the FIFA Match Director shall explain exactly where they may warm up.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 33.5 A maximum of five players (including the goalkeeper) and one official per team shall warm up at the same time next to their own team bench. If there is not sufficient space next to their own team bench, both teams shall warm up behind the advertising boards to the rear of the goal. If there is insufficient space behind the goals, both teams shall warm up in the designated area behind assistant referee no. 1. In this case, three players (including the goalkeeper) and one official per team may warm up at the same time. In all cases, the warm‑up may not involve the use of any balls.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 33.6 Smoking, vaping and the use of any tobacco‑related products are only allowed in the designated areas (if made available) and are not allowed in the technical area, in the vicinity of the field of play or within the competition areas, such as the dressing rooms.

texts.text: Article 34: MATCH PROTOCOL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 34.1 The countdown to kick‑off provided to both teams in advance of the match shall be strictly complied with by both teams. Each team is responsible for arriving at the stadium at the latest 90 minutes before the kick‑off of the respective match. The FIFA flag and the flag of the host country and both competing Participating Member Associations shall be displayed in the stadium at every match in accordance with FIFA's Flag Protocol, unless FIFA states otherwise.

The FIFA flag and the flag of the host country and both competing Participating Member Associations shall be displayed in the stadium at every match in accordance with FIFA's Flag Protocol, unless FIFA states otherwise.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 34.2 Unless FIFA states otherwise, the national anthems of the two teams will be played before each match. The Participating Member Associations shall confirm their national anthem to FIFA (maximum duration of 90 seconds) by the date stipulated in the relevant circular.
texts.text: 34.2 Unless FIFA states otherwise, the national anthems of the two teams will be played before each match. The Participating Member Associations shall confirm their national anthem to FIFA (maximum duration of 90 seconds) by the date stipulated in the relevant circular.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 34.3 The display of political, religious or personal messages or slogans of any nature in any language or form by players and officials is prohibited at any time before the match, during the national anthems, during the match and after the conclusion of the match. The similar display of commercial messages and slogans of any nature in any language or form by players and officials is not allowed for the duration of their time at any official activity organised by FIFA (including official matches and training sessions, as well as during official press conferences and mixed‑zone activities).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 35.1 In order to preserve the quality of the pitch for the matches, no official training sessions will be allowed on the stadium pitches before any match. In principle, all Participating Member Associations' official training sessions will be conducted at the Team Base Camp Training Sites and/or, if applicable, Venue‑Specific Training Sites assigned by FIFA (exemptions to this rule may be granted if pitch conditions allow).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 35.2 Teams that are due to play a match in a stadium in which they have not previously played will be entitled to a familiarisation session in that stadium on either the day before the match or two days before the match. The players and team officials will be permitted to walk around the field of play (to inspect the pitch only and wearing training shoes) and stadium to familiarise themselves with the set‑up.

texts.text: 35.3 Official training session and stadium familiarisation session times will be established and communicated by FIFA.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 35.4 The teams will be entitled to warm up on the pitch before each match, weather and pitch conditions permitting. In principle, such warm‑up will be 30 minutes long and will take place from 50 minutes prior to kick‑off until 20 minutes prior to kick‑off. If the pitch is not in good condition or if warm‑up sessions would negatively affect the state of the pitch for the match, or if the pitch is to be used for ceremonies related to the FIFA World Cup 26, FIFA may shorten or cancel the warm‑up session timings and/or specify restricted areas of use.

texts.text: Article 37: REFEREES

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 37.1 The referees, assistant referees, fourth officials and video match officials (VMOs) (if appointed) (hereinafter referred to collectively as "match officials") for the FIFA World Cup 26 shall be appointed for each match by the FIFA Referees Committee. They shall be selected from the FIFA International Refereeing List in force and come from a member association that is not represented by a team playing in the match in question. A reserve assistant referee shall also be appointed for each match of the final competition. The decisions of the FIFA Referees Committee are final and not subject to appeal.
texts.text: 37.1 The referees, assistant referees, fourth officials and video match officials (VMOs) (if appointed) (hereinafter referred to collectively as "match officials") for the FIFA World Cup 26 shall be appointed for each match by the FIFA Referees Committee. They shall be selected from the FIFA International Refereeing List in force and come from a member association that is not represented by a team playing in the match in question. A reserve assistant referee shall also be appointed for each match of the final competition. The decisions of the FIFA Referees Committee are final and not subject to appeal.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 37.2 If the referee or an assistant referee is prevented from carrying out their duties, such referee or assistant referee shall be replaced by the fourth official and the reserve assistant referee, respectively. The FIFA Referees Committee will determine who replaces a fourth official or a reserve assistant referee who is prevented from carrying out their duties, or who replaces another match official. In exceptional circumstances, the FIFA Referees Committee may decide to proceed with either a fourth official or a reserve assistant referee

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 41.1 Each Participating Member Association shall include at least one team medical doctor (two are recommended) in its delegation, to be present in the technical area during all matches. It is a mandatory requirement that the team medical doctor(s) of each Participating Member Association complete and successfully pass(es) the "Concussion" and "Sudden Cardiac Arrest" modules of the FIFA Diploma in Football Medicine (www.fifamedicalnetwork.com) before the FIFA World Cup 26.

It is a mandatory requirement that the team medical doctor(s) of each Participating Member Association complete and successfully pass(es) the "Concussion" and "Sudden Cardiac Arrest" modules of the FIFA Diploma in Football Medicine (www.fifamedicalnetwork.com) before the FIFA World Cup 26.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 41.2 The team medical doctor is responsible for the overall health of their team delegation and any clinical decision‑making in this regard, both on and off the field of play. Although the team doctors' primary responsibility is to the players, their medical responsibility extends to all other members of the delegation. As such, all team doctors shall ensure that they have the appropriate registration and licence in their home country.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 42.1 Each Participating Member Association shall ensure and confirm to FIFA that its players have undergone a cardiac screening, including personal and family history, clinical examination and resting 12‑lead ECG, within the last 12 months and an echocardiography within the last 24 months prior to the start of the final competition of the FIFA World Cup 26. If specifically performed for the tournament, FIFA recommends that participating teams use the screening form developed by FIFA cardiology consultants.

If specifically performed for the tournament, FIFA recommends that participating teams use the screening form developed by FIFA cardiology consultants.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 42.2 A non‑contact collapse of a player on the field of play should be treated as a sudden cardiac arrest until proven otherwise. The medical team of the team and the field‑of‑play medical team may enter the pitch immediately and commence resuscitation, without waiting for a signal from the referee. One member of the field‑of‑play medical team notifies the fourth official of the collapse and need for cardiopulmonary resuscitation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 42.3 A player who experiences a suspected concussion during a match must undergo an examination by the team medical doctor in accordance with the FIFA Medical Concussion Protocol. The referee may temporarily suspend play for three minutes whenever an incident of suspected concussion occurs. The referee may only allow the player to continue playing with the authorisation of the team medical doctor, who shall have evaluated the player and excluded any suspicion of a concussive injury. The team medical doctor will have the final

The referee may temporarily suspend play for three minutes whenever an incident of suspected concussion occurs. The referee may only allow the player to continue playing with the authorisation of the team medical doctor, who shall have evaluated the player and excluded any suspicion of a concussive injury. The team medical doctor will have the final

texts.label: text
texts.orig: decision based on a clinical examination. Under no circumstances should a player with a suspected concussion return to play.
texts.text: decision based on a clinical examination. Under no circumstances should a player with a suspected concussion return to play.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 42.4 FIFA recommends that medical teams follow the graduated return‑to‑play protocol set forth in the FIFA Concussion Protocol for Medical Staff for any player who has suffered a concussion. The protocol recognises that the time frame for return to play may vary, dependent on factors such as the player's age, gender, their symptoms and medical history, and that doctors must use their clinical judgement in making decisions with respect to return to play. The player's welfare must be their sole concern. FIFA requires that before any player who suffers a concussive episode can return to play, the team medical doctor certify that (a) such player has passed each of the steps set out in the FIFA Concussion Protocol for Medical Staff, (b) such player is fit for competition and (c) the team medical doctor has discussed the management of the case with the FIFA Match Doctor where indicated (subject to consent from the player).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The FIFA Fair Play Award presented by McDonald's is a prize presented to the Participating Member Association finishing first in the fair play contest. FIFA will, on behalf of and in alignment with McDonald's, deliver a football‑related social development (CSR) campaign in the territory of the winning association. texts.label: text
texts.orig: The adidas Golden Boot will be awarded to the player who scores the most goals in the final competition. If two or more players score the same number of goals, the number of assists (as determined by the members of the FIFA Technical Study Group) shall be decisive. If two or more players are still equal after taking into account the number of assists, the total minutes played in the final competition will be taken into account, with the player playing fewer minutes ranked first. The adidas Silver Boot and the adidas Bronze Boot will also be awarded to the second and third‑highest goal scorers, respectively.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: I. GENERAL
texts.text: I. GENERAL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1 As part of its campaign to promote fair play, FIFA regularly holds a fair play contest during its championships, based on an appraisal of the teams' conduct by a FIFA delegate such as the FIFA Match Commissioner, a member of the Technical Study Group or a member of a standing committee.
texts.text: 1 As part of its campaign to promote fair play, FIFA regularly holds a fair play contest during its championships, based on an appraisal of the teams' conduct by a FIFA delegate such as the FIFA Match Commissioner, a member of the Technical Study Group or a member of a standing committee.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2 The objective of fair play activities is to foster a sporting spirit among players, team officials and spectators, thereby heightening the fans' enjoyment of the game.
texts.text: 2 The objective of fair play activities is to foster a sporting spirit among players, team officials and spectators, thereby heightening the fans' enjoyment of the game.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3 At the final whistle of each game, the delegate shall complete the appropriate fair play form after consulting the referee and the referee assessor.
texts.text: 3 At the final whistle of each game, the delegate shall complete the appropriate fair play form after consulting the referee and the referee assessor.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4 Every match in the final competition shall be taken into account.
texts.text: 4 Every match in the final competition shall be taken into account.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5 FIFA shall compile and publish the classification at the end of the final competition. Its decision is final.
texts.text: 5 FIFA shall compile and publish the classification at the end of the final competition. Its decision is final.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6 As a prize awarded to the Participating Member Association that wins the FIFA Fair Play Award presented by McDonald's, FIFA will, on behalf of and in alignment with McDonald's, deliver a football‑related social development (CSR) campaign in the territory of the winning association. The budget for this CSR campaign shall be up to USD 50,000 (fifty thousand US dollars). Both McDonald's and the member association will be consulted on the campaign; however, FIFA reserves the right to determine the nature of the campaign, the dates on which it will take place and the exact location where it will be delivered.
